name: ldap3 (Pure Python LDAP Client)

performance.notes: Network round-trip to LDAP server; connection pooling available

agent_readiness.notes: Pure Python LDAP client for Active Directory and OpenLDAP. from ldap3 import Server, Connection, ALL. server = Server('dc.company.com', get_info=ALL). conn = Connection(server, 'user@company.com', 'password', auto_bind=True). conn.search('dc=company,dc=com', '(objectClass=person)', attributes=['cn', 'mail']). conn.entries. conn.add('cn=user,dc=...', 'inetOrgPerson', {'cn': 'User', 'mail': 'user@...'}). LDAP paging for large result sets. Async mode. AD authentication for Python apps. LDAP injection prevention via filter escaping.

gotchas: AD vs OpenLDAP attribute schemas differ significantly
SSL/TLS certificate validation requires CA cert setup
Paging must be explicitly requested for large result sets
NTLM auth requires python-gssapi or impacket on non-Windows
best_when: Active Directory authentication and user lookup in Python apps
Corporate IT automation interacting with AD
Security tools querying LDAP for user enumeration research
avoid_when: On Windows (python-ldap or WinAPI may be better)
When SAML/OIDC federation is available (prefer over LDAP direct)
Complex LDAP write operations (test thoroughly — AD is unforgiving)
summary: ldap3 is a pure Python LDAP client for Active Directory and OpenLDAP. Connection(server, user, password, auto_bind=True).search(base_dn, filter, attributes) returns entries. Supports NTLM, Kerberos (GSSAPI), simple bind. LDAP paging for large result sets. Async mode available. Used for AD authentication, user sync, and directory operations. Pure Python — no system LDAP lib required.
